paper.title: The time course of in vivo cellular responses to LNPs
paper.authors: Loughrey D, Paunovska K et al.

formulations.ionizable_lipid_reference: Dong et al. PNAS 2014 — known Dahlman lab lipid with published SMILES
formulations.targeting_details: Standard cKK-E12 LNP used for Kupffer cell transcriptomic profiling
formulations.formulation_notes: cKK-E12 ionizable lipid (Dong et al. PNAS 2014). Paper focuses on mechanism (Kupffer cell transcriptomic response) rather than formulation optimization.

experiments.payload: Cre mRNA (tdTomato reporter; 89% Kupffer cell tdTom+ at 72h)

results.liver_metric: Kupffer cell transcriptomic profiling at 1, 3, 6, 24h post-injection
results.liver_notes: CCL7 was the only gene significantly upregulated at ALL FOUR timepoints (1h, 3h, 6h, 24h; p<0.01, FC>2) — this is the Kupffer cell response to the LNP itself. TLR1, TLR4, TLR6 involvement noted. Temporal gene expression progresses from membrane (1h) to nucleus (3h). No significant pro-inflammatory response at any timepoint.

safety.cytokine_elevation: CCL7 upregulated at all timepoints. No significant pro-inflammatory response overall.

experiments.figure_source: Figures in main text (RSC open access)
experiments.notes: Low-input bulk RNA transcriptomic profiling of Kupffer cells. Time-dependent response to LNPs mapped. Key limitation acknowledged by authors: did not establish how Kupffer cell heterogeneity affects delivery.

results.liver_metric: Kupffer cell functional mRNA delivery (protein expression) quantified by flow cytometry
results.liver_notes: Dexamethasone pre-treatment (2.5 mg/kg, 1h prior to LNP): +124% increase in Kupffer cell functional mRNA delivery at 0.3 mg/kg LNP; +82% increase at 1.0 mg/kg LNP. cKK-E12 LNP, aVHH cargo. Mechanism may be improved endosomal escape, not necessarily increased cellular uptake per se. Also increased delivery to some other liver cell types.

safety.tolerability_notes: Dexamethasone is clinically established. Mechanism: suppresses TLR1, TLR6, modulates TLR4. Dex was hypothesized to inhibit CCL7 induction based on prior literature.

experiments.notes: KEY FINDING: Dex pre-treatment INCREASES functional Kupffer cell mRNA delivery (protein expression), not decreases it. +124% at 0.3 mg/kg LNP, +82% at 1.0 mg/kg LNP (2.5 mg/kg dex, 1h prior, cKK-E12 LNP, aVHH cargo). Mechanism may be improved endosomal escape rather than increased cellular uptake. For HSC project: dex pre-treatment does NOT de-target liver. Dex was hypothesized to inhibit CCL7 induction based on prior literature.

key_findings_for_project: Dex pre-treatment increases functional Kupffer cell mRNA delivery (protein expression) by +124% at 0.3 mg/kg LNP and +82% at 1.0 mg/kg LNP (2.5 mg/kg dex 1h prior, cKK-E12 LNP, aVHH cargo)
This is OPPOSITE to the expected liver de-targeting — dex increases functional delivery, possibly via improved endosomal escape
Mechanism: dex suppresses TLR1/TLR4/TLR6 and CCL7, which paradoxically improves LNP functional delivery
CCL7 is the only gene significantly upregulated at ALL FOUR timepoints (1h, 3h, 6h, 24h; p<0.01, FC>2) — this is the response to the LNP itself
Temporal response: membrane effects at 1h, nuclear effects at 3h, resolution by 24h
No significant pro-inflammatory response observed - important safety signal
For HSC project: dex pre-treatment is NOT a liver de-targeting strategy for our purposes

comparison_to_existing_data.vs_breda: Not directly comparable. Breda used CD117-targeted tLNP; this studies untargeted Kupffer cell response.
comparison_to_existing_data.vs_kim: Same lab (Dahlman). Provides mechanistic context for why Kupffer cells capture LNPs and how to modulate this.
comparison_to_existing_data.new_pareto_points: No Pareto data. Mechanistic study only.
comparison_to_existing_data.therapeutic_window_update: Counterintuitive: dex pre-treatment increases functional liver (Kupffer) mRNA delivery (+124% at 0.3 mg/kg, +82% at 1.0 mg/kg). NOT useful for liver de-targeting.
comparison_to_existing_data.gap_formulation_relevance: Minimal direct formulation relevance. Mechanistic insight into Kupffer cell biology useful for understanding liver sink.

limitations_and_caveats: Title in your list ('suppresses Kupffer cell scavenging and enhances LNP delivery') is MISLEADING for our purposes - dex INCREASES Kupffer cell delivery, which means MORE liver uptake
The paper's actual title is 'The time course of in vivo cellular responses to LNPs' - it's a transcriptomics study with dex as a follow-up experiment
Did not deconvolute mRNA cargo vs lipid component effects
Effect on non-liver organs (BM, spleen) not measured
Ionizable lipid is cKK-E12 (Dong et al. PNAS 2014) — known Dahlman lab lipid with published SMILES
